proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_027` greater than or equal to 1 have a lower mean `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_027` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Two-proportion z-test comparing objective_response for feature_027 >= 1 versus feature_027 == 0.
analyses.result_summary: Response was 0.1639 for `feature_027` >= 1 (n=32408) versus 0.2310 for `feature_027` == 0 (n=17592); risk difference -0.0671.

proposed_hypotheses.text: `feature_027` has an ordered negative relationship with `objective_response`, with patients at `feature_027` level 2 having lower response than patients at level 0.

analyses.code: Level-specific response-rate comparison for feature_027 level 2 versus level 0.
analyses.result_summary: Response declined across `feature_027` levels: 0.2310 at level 0 (n=17592), 0.1743 at level 1 (n=24971), and 0.1291 at level 2 (n=7437). Level 2 versus level 0 risk difference was -0.1019.

analyses.result_summary: Top-quartile `feature_004` response was 0.1572 versus 0.2046 in the bottom quartile; risk difference -0.0474.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `feature_015` and `objective_response` is stronger among patients with `feature_025` equal to 1 than among patients with `feature_025` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Risk-difference interaction test: RD(feature_015 | feature_025=1) minus RD(feature_015 | feature_025=0).
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_015` response risk difference was +0.1910 when `feature_025`=1 and -0.0016 when `feature_025`=0; difference-in-differences +0.1926.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `feature_015` and `objective_response` is stronger among patients with `feature_023` equal to 1 than among patients with `feature_023` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Risk-difference interaction test: RD(feature_015 | feature_023=1) minus RD(feature_015 | feature_023=0).
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_015` response risk difference was +0.1476 when `feature_023`=1 and -0.0051 when `feature_023`=0; difference-in-differences +0.1528.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `feature_025` and `objective_response` is stronger among patients with `feature_023` equal to 1 than among patients with `feature_023` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Risk-difference interaction test: RD(feature_025 | feature_023=1) minus RD(feature_025 | feature_023=0).
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_025` response risk difference was +0.1816 when `feature_023`=1 and -0.0092 when `feature_023`=0; difference-in-differences +0.1908.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `feature_025` and `objective_response` is weaker among patients with `feature_001` equal to 1 than among patients with `feature_001` equal to 0.
The positive association between `feature_025` and `objective_response` is weaker among patients with `feature_032` equal to 1 than among patients with `feature_032` equal to 0.

analyses.code: Risk-difference interaction test: RD(feature_025 | feature_001=1) minus RD(feature_025 | feature_001=0).
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_025` response risk difference was -0.0139 when `feature_001`=1 and +0.0819 when `feature_001`=0; difference-in-differences -0.0958.

analyses.code: Risk-difference interaction test: RD(feature_025 | feature_032=1) minus RD(feature_025 | feature_032=0).
analyses.result_summary: The `feature_025` response risk difference was -0.0005 when `feature_032`=1 and +0.0750 when `feature_032`=0; difference-in-differences -0.0755.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The combination `feature_015`=1, `feature_025`=1, and `feature_023`=1 marks a high-response subgroup with higher `objective_response` than the `feature_015`=0, `feature_025`=0, `feature_023`=0 subgroup.

analyses.code: Eight-cell subgroup table for feature_015, feature_025, and feature_023; triple-positive versus triple-negative response-rate comparison.
analyses.result_summary: The triple-positive subgroup had response 0.6331 (1520/2401), while the triple-negative subgroup had response 0.1718 (2153/12533); risk difference +0.4613.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The apparent pairwise interactions among `feature_015`, `feature_025`, and `feature_023` are driven primarily by a positive three-way interaction for patients with all three features equal to 1.

analyses.code: Adjusted linear probability model including main effects for feature_015, feature_025, feature_023, all pairwise products, and the feature_015:feature_025:feature_023 product, plus selected covariates.
analyses.result_summary: After including all lower-order terms and selected main-effect covariates, the `feature_015:feature_025:feature_023` coefficient was +0.4619 on response probability, while pairwise interaction terms were not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for the strongest screened features, `feature_027` greater than or equal to 1 remains independently associated with lower `objective_response`.
After adjustment for the strongest screened features, `feature_004` greater than or equal to 3 remains independently associated with lower `objective_response`.

analyses.code: Adjusted linear probability model with top screened main effects.
analyses.result_summary: In a model including the top eight screened predictors, `feature_027` >= 1 had adjusted coefficient -0.0668.

analyses.code: Adjusted linear probability model with top screened main effects.
analyses.result_summary: In the same model, `feature_004` >= 3 had adjusted coefficient -0.0317.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for the strongest screened features, `feature_031` greater than or equal to 60 remains independently associated with lower `objective_response`.
After adjustment for the strongest screened features, `feature_059` greater than or equal to 3.8 remains independently associated with higher `objective_response`.

analyses.code: Adjusted linear probability model with top screened main effects.
analyses.result_summary: In the top-main-effect adjusted model, `feature_031` >= 60 had adjusted coefficient -0.0257.

analyses.code: Adjusted linear probability model with top screened main effects.
analyses.result_summary: In the top-main-effect adjusted model, `feature_059` >= 3.8 had adjusted coefficient +0.0160.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After modeling the `feature_015`:`feature_025`:`feature_023` three-way interaction, the isolated main effect of `feature_015` is no longer positive and significant.
After modeling the `feature_015`:`feature_025`:`feature_023` three-way interaction, the isolated main effect of `feature_023` is no longer positive and significant.

analyses.code: Adjusted interaction linear probability model with main effects, pairwise terms, three-way term, and selected covariates.
analyses.result_summary: With the three-way term included, the isolated `feature_015` coefficient was -0.0073 and was not significant.

analyses.code: Adjusted interaction linear probability model with main effects, pairwise terms, three-way term, and selected covariates.
analyses.result_summary: With the three-way term included, the isolated `feature_023` coefficient was -0.0095 and was not significant at 0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After modeling the `feature_015`:`feature_025`:`feature_023` three-way interaction, `feature_032` equal to 1 remains independently associated with lower `objective_response`.
After modeling the `feature_015`:`feature_025`:`feature_023` three-way interaction, `feature_053` greater than or equal to 12.2 remains independently associated with lower `objective_response`.

analyses.code: Adjusted interaction linear probability model with selected covariates.
analyses.result_summary: In the interaction-adjusted model, `feature_032`=1 had adjusted coefficient -0.0282.

analyses.code: Adjusted interaction linear probability model with selected covariates.
analyses.result_summary: In the interaction-adjusted model, `feature_053` >= 12.2 had adjusted coefficient -0.0155.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The strongest supported pattern in the cohort is a high-response `objective_response` subgroup defined by `feature_015`=1, `feature_025`=1, and `feature_023`=1, superimposed on independent lower-response associations for `feature_027` >= 1, `feature_004` >= 3, `feature_031` >= 60, `feature_001`=1, `feature_032`=1, and `feature_053` >= 12.2.

analyses.code: Synthesis of univariate tests, quartile contrasts, interaction risk-difference tests, and adjusted linear probability models.
analyses.result_summary: The synthesis was supported: the three-way subgroup coefficient was +0.4619, while independent negative adjusted coefficients persisted for `feature_027` (-0.0674), `feature_004` (-0.0320), `feature_031` (-0.0256), `feature_001` (-0.0242), `feature_032` (-0.0282), and `feature_053` (-0.0155).
